events.label: reasoning
events.text: We need produce JSON answer. Need reason. Scenario: commercial HVAC-R condenser/heat pump setting, observed no service disconnect within sight. Work rough-complete. Need estimate/interpret measurable condition and state why out of tolerance. Reference IMC/IRC M1401. Need know code: IMC M1401? Let's recall. International Mechanical Code M1401 maybe Heating and Cooling Systems? Actually IMC Chapter 14 is Heating and Cooling Systems? M1401.2? Service disconnect? For HVAC equipment, NEC requires disconnect within sight? IMC M1401.3 maybe electrical requirements? Let's recall. International Mechanical Code 2018: Section M1401 Heating and Cooling Systems. M1401.1 General. M1401.2 Hazardous locations. M1401.3 Electrical. Heating, ventilation and air-conditioning systems shall be installed in accordance with the International Residential Code? Wait IRC M1401 is similar. The code likely requires a readily accessible disconnecting means within sight from the appliance? Actually NEC 440.14 requires disconnect within sight from air-conditioning or refrigerating equipment. IMC/IRC M1401.3 says electrical wiring, controls and connections to equipment shall be in accordance with NFPA 70. Also M1401.4 maybe clearances. But scenario explicitly references IMC/IRC M1401. Need apply. No service disconnect within sight is non-compliant. Decision fail. Risk high? Electrical safety, servicing hazard. Work stage rough-complete. Component conditions installed-defective, non-compliant. Percent complete maybe 80? Value? No numeric reading asked; but measurable cue is distance/visibility. Could set value as 0 (no disconnect within sight) or distance unknown. Since no service disconnect, value maybe 0 (number of disconnects within sight). Confidence high. Findings: missing disconnect within sight of condenser/heat pump. Actions: install code-compliant disconnect within sight and readily accessible per NEC/IMC. Rationale. References: IMC/IRC M1401, NEC 440.14 maybe. Need output exactly JSON. Ensure no prose outside.

  "findings": ["No service disconnect is installed within sight of the condenser/heat pump.", "Equipment lacks a readily accessible disconnecting means for safe servicing and emergency shutdown."],
  "actions": ["Install a code-compliant service disconnect within sight and readily accessible from the condenser/heat pump.", "Verify disconnect rating and location with the approved submittals and inspector before close-out."],
  "rationale": "IMC/IRC M1401 requires HVAC equipment electrical connections and disconnecting means to meet code; a service disconnect must be within sight of the appliance so maintenance personnel can safely de-energize it. The observed absence of a disconnect is a clear non-compliance and safety hazard, so the installation fails.",
  "references": ["IMC/IRC M1401", "NEC 440.14"]
}
